vocab.def_en: Having a friendly and pleasant manner; good-natured and likable
vocab.def_zh: 和蔼可亲的;亲切的;友善的
vocab.quote_en: "Such amiable qualities must speak for themselves."
vocab.quote_zh: 这样和蔼可亲的品质本身就是最好的证明。
vocab.ex_en: True amiability is not merely polished manners but a genuine warmth that puts others at ease.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正的和蔼可亲不仅是彬彬有礼的举止，更是一种能让他人感到自在的真诚温暖。

vocab.def_en: A sudden and unaccountable change of mood or behavior; a whim
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Bennet was so odd a mixture of quick parts, sarcastic humor, reserve, and caprice, that the experience of three and twenty years had been insufficient to make his wife understand his character."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特先生是个如此奇特的人——敏捷的才思、讽刺的幽默、矜持寡言、反复无常——以至于二十三年的相处都不足以让他的妻子了解他的性格。
vocab.ex_en: We often mistake the caprice of youth for genuine conviction, only to see it shift like the wind.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们常把年轻人一时的心血来潮误认为真正的信念，结果却发现它如风一般善变。

vocab.def_en: Formal politeness and courtesy in behavior or speech
vocab.quote_en: "'Impossible, Mr. Bennet, impossible, when I am not acquainted with him myself; how can you be so teasing?'"
vocab.quote_zh: “不可能，班纳特先生，不可能，我自己还不认识他呢；你怎么这样会捉弄人？”
vocab.ex_en: Civility costs nothing, yet it can build bridges where pride would erect walls.
vocab.ex_zh: 礼貌无需成本，却能在骄傲筑起高墙之处架起桥梁。

vocab.def_en: The feeling that a person or a thing is worthless or beneath consideration
vocab.quote_en: "I always delight in overthrowing those kind of schemes, and cheating a person of their premeditated contempt."
vocab.quote_zh: 我一向喜欢戳穿这种把戏，让人家的预谋轻蔑落空。
vocab.ex_en: There is no contempt so profound as that which we feel for faults we recognize in ourselves.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有什么轻蔑比我们对自己也有的缺点所感到的轻蔑更为深刻。

vocab.def_en: A person's face or facial expression
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Bingley was good looking and gentlemanlike; he had a pleasant countenance, and easy, unaffected manners."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱先生仪表堂堂，很有绅士风度；他面容可亲，举止从容自然。
vocab.ex_en: A serene countenance often conceals a tempest of thought, just as still waters run deep.
vocab.ex_zh: 平静的面容往往掩盖着思想的波澜，正如静水流深。

vocab.def_en: A person's inherent qualities of mind and character; temperament
vocab.quote_en: "for she had a lively, playful disposition, which delighted in anything ridiculous."
vocab.quote_zh: 因为她生性活泼风趣，对任何可笑的事情都乐在其中。
vocab.ex_en: Our disposition shapes our destiny far more than fortune ever could.
vocab.ex_zh: 决定我们命运的，与其说是运气，不如说是性情。

vocab.def_en: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; difficult to please
vocab.def_zh: 挑剔的;苛求的;过分讲究的
vocab.quote_en: "'I would not be so fastidious as you are,' cried Bingley, 'for a kingdom!'"
vocab.quote_zh: “你就是给我一个王国，我也不愿像你这样挑剔，”彬格莱嚷道。
vocab.ex_en: The fastidious critic of others often proves most lenient when judging his own failings.
vocab.ex_zh: 对别人挑剔的人，评判自己的缺点时往往最为宽容。

vocab.def_en: Showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect
vocab.quote_en: "she could not determine whether the silent contempt of the gentleman, or the insolent smiles of the ladies, were more intolerable."
vocab.quote_zh: 她分辨不出究竟是那位先生的无声轻蔑，还是两位女士的傲慢笑容更让人难以忍受。
vocab.ex_en: Insolence is often the mask that insecurity wears when it ventures into company.
vocab.ex_zh: 傲慢往往是缺乏安全感的人走入人群时所戴的面具。

vocab.def_en: Feeling embarrassed, ashamed, or humiliated
vocab.quote_en: "'That is very true,' replied Elizabeth, 'and I could easily forgive his pride, if he had not mortified mine.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “这倒的确不错，”伊丽莎白回答，“要是他没有伤害我的自尊心，我倒也容易原谅他的骄傲。”
vocab.ex_en: We are never more mortified than when our own weaknesses are exposed by those we secretly admire.
vocab.ex_zh: 当我们暗自钦佩的人揭露了我们的弱点时，那种难堪是无可比拟的。

vocab.def_en: A person one knows slightly, but who is not a close friend; knowledge or experience of something
vocab.quote_en: "'It is very unlucky; but as I have actually paid the visit, we cannot escape the acquaintance now.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “真不走运；可既然我已经去拜访过了，现在就躲不开这门交情了。”
vocab.ex_en: A new acquaintance is like a new book; we cannot judge its worth until we have read a few chapters.
vocab.ex_zh: 新相识如新书，不读几章难知其价值。

vocab.def_en: A reduction in amount, degree, or intensity; the process of becoming less
vocab.quote_en: "The morrow produced no abatement of Mrs. Bennet's ill humor or ill health."
vocab.quote_zh: 第二天，班纳特太太的坏脾气和病痛丝毫没有减轻。
vocab.ex_en: True wisdom comes not from the abatement of life's challenges, but from learning to navigate them with grace.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正的智慧并非来自人生挑战的减少，而是学会优雅地应对它们。

vocab.def_en: The act of washing oneself, often for ritual or hygienic purposes
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Darcy may perhaps have heard of such a place as Gracechurch Street, but he would hardly think a month's ablution enough to cleanse him from its impurities, were he once to enter it."
vocab.quote_zh: 达西先生或许听说过格雷斯彻奇街这样的地方，但若是他踏进那里，恐怕一个月的沐浴也洗不净他沾染的污浊。
vocab.ex_en: The soul requires regular ablution from prejudice, just as the body requires cleansing from daily toil.
vocab.ex_zh: 灵魂需要定期洗去偏见，正如身体需要洗去每日的尘劳。

vocab.def_en: To agree to a demand, request, or treaty; to assume an office or position
vocab.quote_en: "I was perfectly ready to accede to his proposal."
vocab.quote_zh: 我完全准备同意他的提议。
vocab.ex_en: To accede to another's wishes without compromising one's principles is the mark of a generous spirit.
vocab.ex_zh: 在不违背自己原则的前提下同意他人的愿望，是宽厚之心的标志。

vocab.def_en: Bitterness or harshness of temper, manner, or speech
vocab.quote_en: "Gratitude, not merely for having once loved her, but for loving her still well enough to forgive all the petulance and acrimony of her manner in rejecting him."
vocab.quote_zh: 感激，不仅因为他曾经爱过她，更因为尽管她拒绝他时态度那样任性和刻薄，他却依然爱她，足以原谅这一切。
vocab.ex_en: The acrimony of our words often wounds us more deeply than those we aim them at.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们言语的刻薄，往往伤己更深于伤人。

vocab.def_en: To motivate or cause someone to act in a particular way; to put into motion
vocab.quote_en: "all three being actuated by one spirit, everything relating to their journey was speedily settled."
vocab.quote_zh: 三人怀着同一个心愿，于是关于行程的一切都迅速安排妥当。
vocab.ex_en: We are actuated not by circumstance alone, but by the meaning we choose to give it.
vocab.ex_zh: 驱使我们的不仅是环境，更是我们赋予它的意义。

vocab.def_en: To stick firmly to a surface or substance; to believe in and follow the practices of
vocab.quote_en: "Steady to his purpose, he scarcely spoke ten words to her through the whole of Saturday, and though they were at one time left by themselves for half an hour, he adhered most conscientiously to his book, and would not even look at her."
vocab.quote_zh: 他意志坚定，整个星期六几乎没跟她说上十句话，虽然他们曾经有半个钟头的时间单独在一起，他却始终一本正经地看书，看也不看她一眼。
vocab.ex_en: To adhere to one's principles in the face of temptation is the truest test of character.
vocab.ex_zh: 面对诱惑仍坚守原则，是对品格最真实的考验。

vocab.def_en: Friendly, good-natured, and easy to talk to
vocab.def_zh: 和蔼可亲的;友善的;平易近人的
vocab.quote_en: "He is the best landlord, and the best master,' said she, 'that ever lived. Not like the wild young men now-a-days, who think of nothing but themselves. There is not one of his tenants or servants but what will give him a good name. Some people call him proud; but I am sure I never saw any thing of it. To my fancy, it is only because he does not rattle away like other young men.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 她说：“他是最好的地主，最好的主人，从不像现在那些狂野的年轻人，只顾自己。他的佃户和仆人没有一个不说他好话的。有些人说他傲慢，可是我从来没见他有什么傲慢的地方。依我看来，他只是不像其他年轻人那样爱说爱闹罢了。”
vocab.ex_en: An affable disposition opens more doors than the sharpest wit.
vocab.ex_zh: 和蔼可亲的性情比最敏锐的机智更能打开人心。

vocab.def_en: A natural liking or sympathy for someone or something; a close relationship or similarity
vocab.quote_en: "'Undoubtedly,' replied Darcy, to whom this remark was chiefly addressed, 'there is meanness in all the arts which ladies sometimes condescend to employ for captivation. Whatever bears affinity to cunning is despicable.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “毫无疑问，”达西回答，这话主要是对他说的，“凡是姑娘们为了笼络男人所采用的手段，都有下流的地方。凡是和狡诈沾边的东西都是可鄙的。”
vocab.ex_en: We feel affinity not only for those who resemble us, but for those who complete us.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们与之产生共鸣的，不仅是那些与我们相似的人，还有那些使我们完整的人。

vocab.def_en: Brisk and cheerful readiness; eager willingness
vocab.quote_en: "Miss Bingley moved with alacrity to the piano-forte."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱小姐欣然走到钢琴跟前。
vocab.ex_en: Opportunity favors those who greet it with alacrity, not hesitation.
vocab.ex_zh: 机会眷顾那些欣然迎接而非犹豫不决的人。

vocab.def_en: An indirect or passing reference to something; the act of making such a reference
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Collins had a compliment, and an allusion to throw in here, which were kindly smiled on by the mother and daughter."
vocab.quote_zh: 柯林斯先生在这里插了一句恭维话，又暗指了一下，母女俩都对此和蔼地报以微笑。
vocab.ex_en: The most elegant compliment often comes wrapped in a subtle allusion.
vocab.ex_zh: 最优雅的恭维往往包裹在微妙的暗指之中。

vocab.def_en: A minor improvement or change; the act of correcting or improving
vocab.quote_en: "If you, my dear father, will not take the trouble of checking her exuberant spirits, and of teaching her that her present pursuits are not to be the business of her life, she will soon be beyond the reach of amendment."
vocab.quote_zh: 要是你不肯费些心思去管教管教她这种放荡不羁的作风，不教导她明白，她目前的所作所为并不是她一生的正经事，那她马上就会无可救药了。
vocab.ex_en: True amendment requires not merely the recognition of fault, but the will to transform it into wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正的改进不仅需要认识到错误，更需要将错误转化为智慧的意志。

vocab.def_en: Enough or more than enough; plentiful; large in size or capacity
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Bennet's property consisted almost entirely in an estate of two thousand a year, which, unfortunately for his daughters, was entailed in default of heirs male, on a distant relation; and their mother's fortune, though ample for her situation in life, could but ill supply the deficiency of his."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特先生的全部家当几乎都在一宗产业上，每年可以借此获得两千磅的收入。说起这宗产业，真是他女儿们的不幸。他因为没有儿子，产业得由一个远亲来继承，至于她们母亲的家私，在这样的人家本来也算得上一笔大数目，事实上却还不够补他的损失。
vocab.ex_en: An ample fortune may provide comfort, but an ample heart provides happiness.
vocab.ex_zh: 充足的财富可以提供舒适，但宽广的心胸才能带来幸福。

vocab.def_en: A short amusing or interesting story about a real incident or person
vocab.quote_en: "Their powers of conversation were considerable. They could describe an entertainment with accuracy, relate an anecdote with humor, and laugh at their acquaintance with spirit."
vocab.quote_zh: 她们谈锋甚健。她们能够绘声绘色地描述一次宴会，讲起趣闻来妙趣横生，拿熟人开玩笑也兴致勃勃。
vocab.ex_en: A well-told anecdote reveals more about the teller than the tale itself.
vocab.ex_zh: 一个讲得好的故事，透露出的往往比故事本身更多。

vocab.def_en: To add or attach, especially to something larger or more significant
vocab.quote_en: "We will not quarrel for the greater share of blame annexed to that evening," said Elizabeth.
vocab.quote_zh: “我们不必为那天晚上谁更该受责备而争吵了，”伊丽莎白说。
vocab.ex_en: The wisdom we annex from experience becomes the foundation of our character.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们从经验中获得的智慧，成为我们品格的基石。

vocab.def_en: To understand or perceive; to anticipate with fear or anxiety
vocab.quote_en: "and her other sister, from whose disposition greater evil might be apprehended, was likely to be hardened in all her folly and assurance by a situation of such double danger as a watering place and a camp."
vocab.quote_zh: 至于她那另一个妹妹，由于她生性已经埋下了更大的祸根，让她处于浴场和兵营这样双重的危险境地，恐怕她只会更加放荡不羁，更加肆无忌惮。
vocab.ex_en: We often apprehend the faults of others with a clarity that entirely escapes us when examining our own.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们洞察他人缺点时的清晰，审视自己时往往荡然无存。

vocab.def_en: Approval or praise; official recognition or commendation
vocab.quote_en: "She liked him too little to care for his approbation."
vocab.quote_zh: 她太不喜欢他，根本不在乎他的赞许。
vocab.ex_en: The approbation of the wise is worth more than the applause of the multitude.
vocab.ex_zh: 智者的认可，胜过众人的喝彩。

vocab.def_en: With intense feeling or passion; enthusiastically
vocab.quote_en: "'In vain have I struggled. It will not do. My feelings will not be repressed. You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “我挣扎了许久，也是枉然。这办不到。我的感情再也压抑不住了。请允许我告诉你，我多么热烈地倾慕你，爱你。”
vocab.ex_en: That which we ardently desire shapes us as much by its pursuit as by its attainment.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们热烈渴望的东西，追求它的过程塑造我们，一如最终获得它时。

vocab.def_en: An unpaid or overdue debt; an unfinished duty or task
vocab.def_zh: 欠款;未付之债;待办之事
vocab.quote_en: "Miss Bingley was very deeply mortified by Darcy's marriage; but as she thought it advisable to retain the right of visiting at Pemberley, she dropped all her resentment; was fonder than ever of Georgiana, almost as attentive to Darcy as heretofore, and paid off every arrear of civility to Elizabeth."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱小姐对于达西的结婚很是气愤，可是，既然她想要仍旧在彭伯里消受作客的乐趣，那就不妨把一切气愤都打消了。她对乔治安娜比以前更亲热，对达西几乎跟从前一样殷勤，此外，她对伊丽莎白又礼貌周全，连以前欠她的礼也弥补过来了。
vocab.ex_en: The heart keeps an arrear of kindnesses owed, and finds its richest payment in giving.
vocab.ex_zh: 心中铭记着亏欠的善意，而在付出时得到最丰厚的回报。

vocab.def_en: To find out or learn with certainty; to determine
vocab.quote_en: "The ladies were somewhat more fortunate, for they had the advantage of ascertaining from an upper window that he wore a blue coat and rode a black horse."
vocab.quote_zh: 几位小姐倒是比较幸运，她们可以从楼上的窗口看清楚他穿的是蓝外套，骑的是一匹黑马。
vocab.ex_en: To ascertain truth requires not only diligent inquiry but the humility to abandon cherished misconceptions.
vocab.ex_zh: 确定真理不仅需要勤奋探究，更需要放弃珍视的误解的谦逊。

vocab.def_en: Harshness of tone or manner; roughness or sharpness
vocab.quote_en: "But here he was set right by Mrs. Bennet, who assured him with some asperity that they were very well able to keep a good cook, and that her daughters had nothing to do in the kitchen."
vocab.quote_zh: 这话却给班纳特太太纠正了，她带着几分严厉地说，她们家完全雇得起一个好厨子，女儿们根本和厨房沾不上边。
vocab.ex_en: The asperity of our judgments often reflects the severity of our own inner critic.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们评判的严厉，往往反映了我们内心对自己的苛责。

vocab.def_en: To agree or have the same opinion; to happen at the same time
vocab.quote_en: "My overhearings were more to the purpose than yours, Eliza,' said Charlotte."
vocab.quote_zh: “我听到的话比你的更有意思，伊莱札，”夏绿蒂说。
vocab.ex_en: When hearts and minds concur, even the most difficult paths become journeyable.
vocab.ex_zh: 当心意相通，再艰难的路也变得可行。

vocab.def_en: Warm and friendly; heartfelt; sincere
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth remained with no very cordial feelings towards him."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白对他实在没有什么好感。
vocab.ex_en: A cordial reception costs little but enriches both giver and receiver immeasurably.
vocab.ex_zh: 热诚的接待不费分文，却使给予者和接受者都获益匪浅。

vocab.def_en: The ability to judge well; keen perception or insight
vocab.quote_en: "and with more quickness of observation and less pliancy of temper than her sister, and with a judgment too unassailed by any attention to herself, she was very little disposed to approve them."
vocab.quote_zh: 她比姐姐观察力更敏锐，性情也不像姐姐那样随和，再加上她本人没有受到任何青睐，因此她对那两位女士颇不以为然。
vocab.ex_en: Discernment is the art of seeing beyond what is presented to what truly is.
vocab.ex_zh: 洞察力是看穿表象、洞悉本质的艺术。

vocab.def_en: Intense happiness; the ability to find appropriate expression for one's thoughts
vocab.quote_en: "If the dispositions of the parties are ever so well known to each other, or ever so similar beforehand, it does not advance their felicity in the least."
vocab.quote_zh: 如果双方事先对彼此的性格就非常了解，或者非常相似，那也丝毫不会增进他们的幸福。
vocab.ex_en: The pursuit of felicity is the most universal yet most individual of human endeavors.
vocab.ex_zh: 追求幸福是人类最普遍却又最个性化的努力。

vocab.def_en: Patient self-control; restraint and tolerance
vocab.quote_en: "He was struggling for the appearance of composure, and would not open his lips, till he believed himself to have attained it. The pause was to Elizabeth's feelings dreadful. At length, in a voice of forced calmness, he said..."
vocab.quote_zh: 他竭力装出镇定的样子，一直闭口不响，直等他觉得自己已经达到了这个境界，然后才开口说话。这阵沉默使伊丽莎白感到非常难受。最后，他强作镇定地说道：
vocab.ex_en: Forbearance in the face of provocation is not weakness but the highest form of strength.
vocab.ex_zh: 面对挑衅时的克制不是软弱，而是最高形式的坚强。

vocab.def_en: Not showing proper respect; rude; irrelevant
vocab.def_zh: 无礼的;粗鲁的;不相干的
vocab.quote_en: "I will take care of myself, and of Mr. Wickham too. He shall not be in love with me, if I can prevent it."
vocab.quote_zh: “我会照顾我自己，也会照顾韦翰先生。要是我能阻止他爱我，我一定不让他爱。”
vocab.ex_en: The impertinent question often reveals more about the asker than the answer ever could about the subject.
vocab.ex_zh: 无礼的问题往往暴露提问者，甚于答案揭示真相。

vocab.def_en: Beginning to happen or develop; in an early stage
vocab.def_zh: 初期的;早期的;刚出现的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth listened in silence, but was not convinced. Their behavior at the assembly had not been calculated to please in general."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白没有作声，可是心里并没有被说服。她们在舞会上的行为举止，本来就不打算讨众人喜欢。
vocab.ex_en: The incipient stages of affection are the most delicate, requiring neither declaration nor acknowledgment to flourish.
vocab.ex_zh: 感情的萌芽阶段最为微妙，无需表白也无需承认，自会生长。

vocab.def_en: Unfair bias in favor of one thing or person; a particular liking
vocab.quote_en: "It was generally evident whenever they met, that he did admire her; and to her it was equally evident that Jane was yielding to the preference which she had begun to entertain for him from the first, and was in a way to be very much in love."
vocab.quote_zh: 他们每次见面，总是他越来越爱慕她，这在大家看来都很明显；在伊丽莎白看来，她姐姐已经对他有了好感，而且开头开得很好，一定会对他倾心相爱。
vocab.ex_en: Partiality in love is not injustice but the heart's natural gravitation toward its proper orbit.
vocab.ex_zh: 爱情中的偏爱并非不公，而是心灵对其应有轨道的自然吸引。

vocab.def_en: Deserving of criticism or disapproval; blameworthy
vocab.def_zh: 应受谴责的;该受指责的;不道德的
vocab.quote_en: "The idea of Mr. Collins, with all his solemn composure, being run away with by his feelings, made Elizabeth so near laughing that she could not use the short pause he allowed in any attempt to stop him farther."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白看到他那副一本正经的样子，听到他竟然会被感情冲动，差点儿笑出声来，因此他停下来喘口气的那片刻工夫，她也不想拦住他再说下去。
vocab.ex_en: Actions may be reprehensible, but understanding their roots requires compassion rather than condemnation.
vocab.ex_zh: 行为或许该受谴责，但要理解其根源，需要的是同情而非指责。

vocab.def_en: Great force or intensity of feeling, expression, or action
vocab.quote_en: "To be sure, he had not written with such vehemence as to make her suppose he had any design of obtaining her hand."
vocab.quote_zh: 当然，他信里措辞并不十分热烈，决不会使她猜想他有什么向她求婚的打算。
vocab.ex_en: The vehemence of our convictions should be matched by the gentleness of our expression.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们信念的热烈程度，应与我们表达的温和程度相匹配。

vocab.def_en: A feeling of repulsion; disgusted loathing
vocab.quote_en: "The sight of Miss Lucas was odious to her. As her successor in that house, she regarded her with jealous abhorrence."
vocab.quote_zh: 她一看到卢卡斯小姐就讨厌。一想到这个房子今后的继承人就是她，她就把她看作眼中钉。
vocab.ex_en: True abhorrence of evil must begin with recognition of its presence within ourselves.
vocab.ex_zh: 对邪恶的真正憎恶，必须始于认识到它存在于我们自己内心。

vocab.def_en: To accept something reluctantly but without protest
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth was silent, but her force of belief was not to be immediately acquiesced in."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白没有作声，可是她并没有因此就信服。
vocab.ex_en: Wisdom often requires us to acquiesce to what we cannot change, while finding courage to transform what we can.
vocab.ex_zh: 智慧常常要求我们默许无法改变之事，同时鼓起勇气改变能够改变之事。

vocab.def_en: With intense feeling or passion; enthusiastically
vocab.quote_en: "'In vain have I struggled. It will not do. My feelings will not be repressed. You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “我挣扎了许久，也是枉然。这办不到。我的感情再也压抑不住了。请允许我告诉你，我多么热烈地倾慕你，爱你。”
vocab.ex_en: That which we ardently desire shapes us as much by its pursuit as by its attainment.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们热烈渴望的东西，追求它的过程塑造我们，一如最终获得它时。

vocab.def_en: Clever or cunning devices or expedients, especially as used to trick or deceive
vocab.quote_en: "There is so much of gratitude or vanity in almost every attachment, that it is not safe to leave any to itself. We can all begin freely—a slight preference is natural enough; but there are very few of us who have heart enough to be really in love without encouragement."
vocab.quote_zh: 几乎所有的恋爱里都有那么多的感激或虚荣心，所以听其自然是最不保险的。开头大家都很随便——略为有点好感本是很自然的事，但是很少人有足够的心肠真正恋爱而不加鼓励。
vocab.ex_en: The highest artifice is to appear without artifice, and the deepest sincerity needs no disguise.
vocab.ex_zh: 最高明的技巧是看起来毫无技巧，最深沉的真诚无需伪装。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being open, honest, and sincere in expression
vocab.quote_en: "Affectation of candor is common enough;—one meets it everywhere. But to be candid without ostentation or design—to take the good of everybody's character and make it still better, and say nothing of the bad—belongs to you alone."
vocab.quote_zh: 装模作样的坦率是再普通不过的事——到处都能碰到。可是，不摆架子、不存心机地坦率——只看到每个人的优点，把它说得更好，对缺点绝口不提——这只有你才做得到。
vocab.ex_en: Candor without kindness is merely cruelty in transparent clothing.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有善意的坦率，不过是披着透明外衣的残忍。

vocab.def_en: A sudden and unaccountable change of mood or behavior; a whim
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Bennet was so odd a mixture of quick parts, sarcastic humor, reserve, and caprice, that the experience of three and twenty years had been insufficient to make his wife understand his character."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特先生是个如此奇特的人——敏捷的才思、讽刺的幽默、矜持寡言、反复无常——以至于二十三年的相处都不足以让他的妻子了解他的性格。
vocab.ex_en: We often mistake the caprice of youth for genuine conviction, only to see it shift like the wind.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们常把年轻人一时的心血来潮误认为真正的信念，结果却发现它如风一般善变。

vocab.def_en: Swiftness of movement; speed
vocab.quote_en: "I dare say you believed it; but I am by no means convinced that you would be gone with such celerity."
vocab.quote_zh: 我敢说你相信这点；可是我决不相信你真会走得那么快。
vocab.ex_en: The celerity with which we judge others is often exceeded only by the slowness with which we examine ourselves.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们评判他人的迅速，往往只有我们审视自己的迟缓能与之相比。

vocab.def_en: The expression of formal disapproval; harsh criticism
vocab.quote_en: "I would wish not to be hasty in censuring any one; but I always speak what I think."
vocab.quote_zh: 我并不想轻易指责别人；可是我总说老实话。
vocab.ex_en: Hasty censure reveals more about the critic than the criticized.
vocab.ex_zh: 仓促的指责更多地暴露了批评者而非被批评者。

vocab.def_en: The fact or process of ending or being brought to an end
vocab.quote_en: "Such doings discomposed Mr. Bennet exceedingly. In his library he had been always sure of leisure and tranquillity; and though prepared, as he told Elizabeth, to meet with folly and conceit in every other room in the house, he was used to be free from them there."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特先生给弄得极其不安。他原在书房里可以安安逸逸，悠闲自得，他告诉伊丽莎白说，他到别的房间里去，准备碰到愚蠢和自负，可是书房里却没有这种事。
vocab.ex_en: Without cessation of our internal noise, we cannot hear the quiet voice of wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 如果不停止内心的喧嚣，我们就无法听见智慧宁静的声音。

vocab.def_en: Distress or embarrassment at having failed or been humiliated
vocab.quote_en: "In her own past behavior, there was a constant source of vexation and regret; and in the unhappy defects of her family a subject of yet heavier chagrin."
vocab.quote_zh: 回顾起自己过去的行为，她越想越觉得又烦恼又悔恨；家庭的缺陷更使她感到苦闷。
vocab.ex_en: Chagrin is the bitter fruit of hindsight, teaching us what we wish we had known before.
vocab.ex_zh: 懊恼是后见之明的苦果，教会我们但愿早知之事。

vocab.def_en: Wary and unwilling to take risks; prudent
vocab.quote_en: "We must not be so ready to fancy ourselves intentionally injured. We must not expect a lively young man to be always so guarded and circumspect."
vocab.quote_zh: 我们不要老是想到自己受了人家的骗。我们不能指望一个活泼的年轻人会始终小心周到。
vocab.ex_en: To be circumspect is not to live in fear, but to walk with eyes open through a world of shadows.
vocab.ex_zh: 谨慎不是活在恐惧中，而是在阴影遍布的世界里睁大眼睛行走。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being wary and unwilling to take risks; prudence
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Darcy's behavior astonished and vexed her. 'Why, if he came only to be silent, grave, and indifferent,' said she, 'did he come at all?'"
vocab.quote_zh: 达西先生的行为使她诧异，也使她烦恼。“要是他来了只是为了要默不作声，板着脸，冷冷淡淡，”她说，“那他何必来呢？”
vocab.ex_en: Circumspection in speech is the wisdom that knows when silence serves better than words.
vocab.ex_zh: 言语谨慎是一种智慧，知道何时沉默胜于千言万语。

vocab.def_en: Clear, logical, and convincing
vocab.def_zh: 有说服力的;令人信服的;有力的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, to whom Jane very soon communicated the chief of all this, heard it in silent indignation."
vocab.quote_zh: 吉英立刻把信里最重要的部分告诉了伊丽莎白，伊丽莎白听了默不作声，只是心里气愤。
vocab.ex_en: The most cogent arguments often fail against the fortress of determined ignorance.
vocab.ex_zh: 最有说服力的论点，也常常无法攻破顽固无知筑起的堡垒。

vocab.def_en: Sympathy and sorrow for the misfortunes of others; compassion
vocab.quote_en: "On his quitting the room, she sat down, unable to support herself, and looking so miserably ill that it was impossible for Darcy to leave her, or to refrain from saying, in a tone of gentleness and commiseration, 'Let me call your maid. Is there nothing you could take, to give you present relief?—A glass of wine;—shall I get you one?—You are very ill.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 他走出房间后，她坐下来，支持不住，脸色非常难看，使达西无法离开她，只得用一种温柔体贴的声调跟她说：“让我去叫你的女佣人吧。你能不能吃点东西，让你好过一些？——要不要给你拿一杯酒？——你病得很厉害。”
vocab.ex_en: Commiseration without action is merely sentiment; compassion made real reaches out to heal.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有行动的同情只是空泛的情感；化作行动的慈悲才是伸手医治。

vocab.def_en: A feeling of smug or uncritical satisfaction with oneself or one's achievements
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, easy and unaffected, had been listened to with much more pleasure, though not playing half so well; and Mary, at the end of a long concerto, was glad to purchase praise and gratitude by Scotch and Irish airs, at the request of her younger sisters, who with some of the Lucases and two or three officers joined eagerly in dancing at one end of the room."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白弹得虽然远不如她，可是落落大方，没有矫揉造作的气派，因此大家听起来就高兴得多了。曼丽奏完了一支很长的协奏曲之后，她的妹妹们便要求她再奏几支苏格兰和爱尔兰小调，她也高高兴兴地照办了，为的是要博得别人的夸奖和感激。她的几位妹妹和卢家的一些姐妹便跟着一位军官，在房间的一头蹦跳起来。
vocab.ex_en: Complacency is the quiet enemy of growth, whispering that we have arrived when we have only begun.
vocab.ex_zh: 自满是成长的隐形敌人，在我们刚刚起步时就悄悄告诉我们已经抵达终点。

vocab.def_en: Willing to please others; obliging; agreeable
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Collins had only to change from Jane to Elizabeth—and it was soon done—done while Mrs. Bennet was stirring the fire."
vocab.quote_zh: 柯林斯先生只得从吉英改选到伊丽莎白——而且一下子就选定了——就在班纳特太太拨火的那一忽儿工夫里选定的。
vocab.ex_en: A complaisant manner often conceals the most determined will.
vocab.ex_zh: 殷勤的态度往往隐藏着最坚定的意志。

vocab.def_en: To stop someone from being angry or discontented; to placate or pacify
vocab.quote_en: "His plan did not vary on seeing them.—Miss Bennet's lovely face confirmed his views, and established all his strictest notions of what was due to seniority; and for the first evening she was his settled choice."
vocab.quote_zh: 他见到她们之后，计划并没有改变。吉英的美丽面孔早已把他的心抓住了，因此他断定她应得享受大女儿的权利。
vocab.ex_en: To conciliate is not to surrender but to choose peace over victory.
vocab.ex_zh: 安抚不是投降，而是选择和平而非胜利。

vocab.def_en: Tending to make peace; pacifying
vocab.def_zh: 安抚的;调和的;赢得人心的
vocab.quote_en: "Her air was not conciliating, nor was her manner of receiving them, such as to make her visitors forget their inferior rank."
vocab.quote_zh: 她的气派并不十分亲切，接待宾客的态度也不能使宾客忘却自己身份的低微。
vocab.ex_en: A conciliating spirit builds bridges where walls once stood.
vocab.ex_zh: 调和的精神在有墙之处架起桥梁。

vocab.def_en: Intended or likely to placate or pacify
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Darcy felt their rudeness and immediately said, 'This walk is not wide enough for our party. We had better go into the avenue.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 达西先生觉得她们太冒昧了，便立刻说道：“这条路太窄，我们大伙儿挤不下，不如到大道上去吧。”
vocab.ex_en: A conciliatory word at the right moment can heal wounds that time alone cannot mend.
vocab.ex_zh: 适时的和解之言，能治愈时间无法弥合的伤口。

vocab.def_en: Relating to marriage or the relationship of spouses
vocab.quote_en: "She began now to comprehend that he was exactly the man, who, in disposition and talents, would most suit her. His understanding and temper, though unlike her own, would have answered all her wishes. It was a union that must have been to the advantage of both."
vocab.quote_zh: 她如今才开始明白，他无论在个性方面和才能方面，都百分之百是一个最适合她的男人。纵使他的见解，他的脾气，和她自己不是一模一样，可是一定能够叫她称心如意。这个结合对双方都有好处。
vocab.ex_en: Connubial bliss is not found in perfection but in the daily choice to love despite imperfection.
vocab.ex_zh: 婚姻的幸福不在于找到完美，而在于日日选择去爱不完美。

vocab.def_en: Feelings of anxiety or dismay, typically at something unexpected
vocab.quote_en: "When Mr. Collins could be forgotten, there was really a great air of comfort throughout, and by Charlotte's evident enjoyment of it, Elizabeth supposed he must be often forgotten."
vocab.quote_zh: 只要把柯林斯先生忘掉，这儿便极其舒适。伊丽莎白看到夏绿蒂那样得意，便不得不想到她一定是常常把柯林斯先生忘掉的。
vocab.ex_en: Consternation seizes us when the world refuses to conform to our expectations.
vocab.ex_zh: 当世界拒绝符合我们的期望时，惊愕便攫住了我们。

vocab.def_en: To interpret or understand in a particular way
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, construing all this into a wish of hearing her speak of her sister, was pleased; and on this account, as well as some others, found herself, when their visitors left them, capable of considering the last half hour with some satisfaction."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白把这一切都看作是他想要听她谈谈自己姐姐的心事，因此心里很是高兴；就为了这个缘故，等到他们走了以后，她竟然觉得自己刚才跟他在一起待的那半个钟头，不能说完全不满意。
vocab.ex_en: We construe the actions of others through the lens of our own hearts, seeing what we are prepared to see.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们通过自己心灵的镜头解读他人的行为，看到的只是我们准备看到的东西。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being contrary; opposition or inconsistency
vocab.quote_en: "If Elizabeth, when Mr. Darcy gave her the letter, did not expect it to contain a renewal of his offers, she had formed no expectation at all of its contents. But such as they were, it may be well supposed how eagerly she went through them, and what a contrariety of emotion they excited."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白当初接到达西先生的信时，万万想不到信中内容会重新提出他的求婚，因此她根本没有预料到信里会写些什么。既然是这样的信，不难想象，她读的时候该是多么专心，心里又给引起了多少矛盾的感觉。
vocab.ex_en: The human heart is a theater of contrariety, where love and hate perform upon the same stage.
vocab.ex_zh: 人心是矛盾之戏的剧场，爱与恨在同一舞台上演。

vocab.def_en: The use of skill to create or bring about something; a clever plan or device
vocab.quote_en: "This was a lucky idea of mine, indeed!' said Mrs. Bennet, more than once, as if the credit of making it rain were all her own. Till the next morning, however, she was not aware of all the felicity of her contrivance."
vocab.quote_zh: “我这个办法的确想得巧妙！”班纳特太太不止一次这样说，仿佛下雨也全是她一手造成的。不过，她一直到第二天早晨才知道，她的巧妙办法造成了多大的幸福。
vocab.ex_en: The best contrivances are those so natural they appear as no contrivance at all.
vocab.ex_zh: 最高明的计谋是那些如此自然，以至于看起来根本不是计谋。

vocab.def_en: To plan or devise skillfully; to bring about by planning
vocab.quote_en: "They always contrive to grow sufficiently unlike afterwards to have their share of vexation; and it is better to know as little as possible of the defects of the person with whom you are to pass your life."
vocab.quote_zh: 婚后的生活总会生出些烦恼，让你先前了解得不够，后来就够你受的。你既然要和一个人过一辈子，对他的缺点知道得越少越好。
vocab.ex_en: We contrive our futures daily through the small choices that become the architecture of our lives.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们每日通过细小的选择策划自己的未来，这些选择成为我们生命的建筑。

vocab.def_en: To deny the truth of something; to dispute or oppose
vocab.quote_en: "delivering her opinion on every subject in so decisive a manner as proved that she was not used to have her judgment controverted."
vocab.quote_zh: 对每个问题都带着那么果断的语气发表意见，足见她是个惯于发号施令的人。
vocab.ex_en: The truth need not fear to be controverted; it stands firm while falsehood crumbles.
vocab.ex_zh: 真理不怕被反驳；它屹立不倒，而谬误终将瓦解。

vocab.def_en: A small group of trees
vocab.quote_en: "Upon this information, they instantly passed through the hall once more, and ran across the lawn after their father, who was deliberately pursuing his way towards a small wood on one side of the paddock."
vocab.quote_zh: 一听到这话，她们立刻再一次穿过厅堂，跑过草地，追赶父亲，他正从容不迫地向围场边的小树林走去。
vocab.ex_en: In the quiet of the copse, away from society's noise, truth often speaks most clearly.
vocab.ex_zh: 在远离社会喧嚣的小树林的寂静中，真理常常说得最清楚。

vocab.def_en: Flirtatious behavior; a woman's exploitation of her charms to gain attention
vocab.quote_en: "He assured her that as to dancing, he was perfectly indifferent to it; that his chief object was by delicate attentions to recommend himself to her, and that he should therefore make a point of remaining close to her the whole evening."
vocab.quote_zh: 他告诉她，他对跳舞简直无所谓，他的主要目的是要向她献殷勤，因此他决定整个晚上都陪伴在她身边。
vocab.ex_en: Coquetry is the art of promising without giving, of hinting without declaring.
vocab.ex_zh: 卖俏是许诺而不给予的艺术，是暗示而不明言。

vocab.def_en: Warm and friendly; heartfelt; sincere
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth remained with no very cordial feelings towards him."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白对他实在没有什么好感。
vocab.ex_en: A cordial reception costs little but enriches both giver and receiver immeasurably.
vocab.ex_zh: 热诚的接待不费分文，却使给予者和接受者都获益匪浅。

vocab.def_en: A person's face or facial expression
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Bingley was good looking and gentlemanlike; he had a pleasant countenance, and easy, unaffected manners."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱先生仪表堂堂，很有绅士风度；他面容可亲，举止从容自然。
vocab.ex_en: A serene countenance often conceals a tempest of thought, just as still waters run deep.
vocab.ex_zh: 平静的面容往往掩盖着思想的波澜，正如静水流深。

vocab.def_en: To reduce in extent or quantity; to cut short
vocab.quote_en: "a letter arrived from Mrs. Gardiner, which at once delayed its commencement and curtailed its extent."
vocab.quote_zh: 嘉丁纳太太来了一封信，一开头就使这件事耽搁下来，而且还缩小了游览的范围。
vocab.ex_en: We curtail our own possibilities more often by fear than by circumstance.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们往往因恐惧而非环境，缩减了自己的可能性。

vocab.def_en: Behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety; etiquette
vocab.quote_en: "To walk three miles, or four miles, or five miles, or whatever it is, above her ankles in dirt, and alone, quite alone! what could she mean by it? It seems to me to show an abominable sort of conceited independence, a most country town indifference to decorum."
vocab.quote_zh: 让她跑了三四英里路，跑得脚上沾满了肮脏的泥泞，孤孤单单的一个人！这究竟是什么意思？我看她好像毫无家教的野人，完全不懂得上流社会的规矩。
vocab.ex_en: Decorum without heart is mere performance; true grace flows from within.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有心的礼节只是表演；真正的优雅从内流出。

vocab.def_en: Humble submission and respect; courteous regard
vocab.quote_en: "I assure you that if Darcy were not such a great tall fellow, in comparison with myself, I should not pay him half so much deference."
vocab.quote_zh: 我老实告诉你，要不是达西比我长得高那么多，我才不会对他那么尊敬呢。
vocab.ex_en: Deference paid to rank alone is flattery; deference paid to character is wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 只对地位表示尊敬是谄媚；对品格表示尊敬是智慧。

vocab.def_en: To do something that one considers beneath one's dignity; to condescend
vocab.quote_en: "Mrs. Bennet deigned not to make any reply; but unable to contain herself, began scolding one of her daughters."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特太太没有回答，可是她实在忍不住了，便骂起女儿来。
vocab.ex_en: Greatness is measured not by how high one can climb, but by how low one will deign to stoop in service to others.
vocab.ex_zh: 伟大不取决于能攀多高，而取决于肯屈尊多低去服务他人。

vocab.def_en: The way a person stands and walks; a person's bearing or behavior
vocab.quote_en: "When, after examining the mother, in whose countenance and deportment she soon found some resemblance of Mr. Darcy, she turned her eyes on the daughter, she could almost have joined in Maria's astonishment at her being so thin, and so small."
vocab.quote_zh: 她把那位母亲打量了一番，觉得她的容貌和风度跟达西先生十分相像，然后又去看她的女儿，她简直跟玛丽亚一样惊奇，只见她长得那么单薄，那么瘦小。
vocab.ex_en: True deportment is not the art of appearing, but the natural expression of being.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正的举止不是表现的艺术，而是存在的自然流露。

vocab.def_en: Moral corruption; wickedness
vocab.quote_en: "Willfully and wantonly to have thrown off the companion of my youth, the acknowledged favorite of my father, a young man who had scarcely any other dependence than on our patronage, and who had been brought up to expect its exertion, would be a depravity, to which the separation of two young persons, whose affection could be the growth of only a few weeks, could bear no comparison."
vocab.quote_zh: 存心把幼年的伙伴、父亲所宠爱的人、一个除了依靠我们家庭以外别无依靠的人，蓄意抛弃，使他走投无路，这真是天大的罪过，跟那桩事比起来，破坏一对年轻恋人几星期的私情，简直算不了一回事。
vocab.ex_en: To recognize depravity in others without seeing it in ourselves is the deepest depravity of all.
vocab.ex_zh: 识别他人身上的堕落而不见自己身上的，乃是最深的堕落。

vocab.def_en: Contemptuous ridicule or mockery
vocab.quote_en: "She looked at Jane, to see how she bore it; but Jane was very composedly talking to Bingley. She looked at his two sisters, and saw them making signs of derision at each other."
vocab.quote_zh: 她望望吉英，看看吉英受不受得了。只见吉英正安安静静地在跟彬格莱先生攀谈。她又望望彬格莱的两位姐妹，只见双方正在挤眉弄眼地讥笑。
vocab.ex_en: Derision is the weapon of those who cannot engage with ideas, only with their contempt for them.
vocab.ex_zh: 嘲弄是那些无法与思想对话、只能对其表示轻蔑者的武器。

vocab.def_en: Entirely lacking or free from
vocab.quote_en: "You thought me then devoid of every proper feeling, I am sure you did."
vocab.quote_zh: 我相信你当时一定以为我完全没有应有的情感。
vocab.ex_en: A life devoid of reflection is a life only half-lived.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有反思的人生，只是活了一半的人生。

vocab.def_en: Tending to dictate; domineering; autocratic
vocab.quote_en: "She has the reputation of being remarkably sensible and clever: but I rather believe she derives part of her abilities from her rank and fortune, part from her authoritative manner, and the rest from the pride of her nephew, who chooses that everyone connected with him should have an understanding of the first class."
vocab.quote_zh: 大家都说她非常能干，非常聪明，可是我倒认为，她的能干一部分来自于她的地位和财产，一部分来自于她那种专横的态度，其余部分则来自于她外甥的骄傲，因为她的外甥一定要每一个跟他有关系的人都具有一流的心智。
vocab.ex_en: A dictatorial spirit reveals insecurity masquerading as certainty.
vocab.ex_zh: 专横的精神是伪装成确定的缺乏安全感。

vocab.def_en: Modesty or shyness resulting from a lack of self-confidence
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth admired the command of countenance with which Charlotte talked of the healthfulness of the exercise, and owned she encouraged it as much as possible."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白看到夏绿蒂谈起这事时不动声色，便不禁暗暗赞叹她善于控制自己的脸色。
vocab.ex_en: Diffidence often conceals depths that confidence cannot fathom.
vocab.ex_zh: 羞怯往往隐藏着自信无法测量的深度。

vocab.def_en: Modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence
vocab.def_zh: 缺乏自信的;羞怯的;谦逊的
vocab.quote_en: "He thought too well of himself to comprehend on what motive his cousin could refuse him; and though his pride was hurt, he suffered in no other way. His regard for her was quite imaginary; and the possibility of her deserving her mother's reproach prevented his feeling any regret."
vocab.quote_zh: 他把自己估价太高了，因此弄不明白表妹所以拒绝他，究竟是什么动机。虽说他自尊心受了伤，此外他并不觉得有什么难过。他对她的一片好意纯粹是凭空想象的，既然事实上她似乎辜负了他这片好意，那也只得罢了。
vocab.ex_en: The diffident soul underestimates its worth, while the arrogant overestimates its wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 缺乏自信者低估自己的价值，而傲慢者高估自己的智慧。

vocab.def_en: Slow to act; intended to cause delay
vocab.quote_en: "His family knew him to be, on all common occasions, a most negligent and dilatory correspondent, but at such a time, they had hoped for exertion."
vocab.quote_zh: 家里人都知道，他平常总是个最懒得动笔的人，可是这一次她们却希望他能勉为其难。
vocab.ex_en: The dilatory heart postpones today what should be done, forgetting that tomorrow may never come.
vocab.ex_zh: 拖延的心把今天该做的推给明天，却忘了明天也许永远不会来。

vocab.def_en: A reduction in the size, extent, or importance of something
vocab.quote_en: "Their first subject was the diminution of the Rosings party."
vocab.quote_zh: 她们一见面就谈到罗新斯的宴会减少了。
vocab.ex_en: The diminution of pride is the first step toward the growth of wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 骄傲的减少是智慧增长的第一步。

vocab.def_en: Strong disapproval, typically on moral grounds
vocab.quote_en: "I am sorry, exceedingly sorry,' replied Darcy, in a tone of surprise and emotion, 'that you have ever been informed of what may, in a mistaken light, have given you uneasiness.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “我很抱歉，非常抱歉，”达西先生用惊讶激动的声调说，“你竟会听到那些事，这一定会使你感到不安，也许让你对事情有了错误的看法。”
vocab.ex_en: Disapprobation from the wise should give us pause; from the foolish, it is a badge of honor.
vocab.ex_zh: 智者的不赞成应使我们反思；愚者的不赞成，则是荣誉的徽章。

vocab.def_en: The ability to judge well; keen perception or insight
vocab.quote_en: "and with more quickness of observation and less pliancy of temper than her sister, and with a judgment too unassailed by any attention to herself, she was very little disposed to approve them."
vocab.quote_zh: 她比姐姐观察力更敏锐，性情也不像姐姐那样随和，再加上她本人没有受到任何青睐，因此她对那两位女士颇不以为然。
vocab.ex_en: Discernment is the art of seeing beyond what is presented to what truly is.
vocab.ex_zh: 洞察力是看穿表象、洞悉本质的艺术。

vocab.def_en: Disturbed or unsettled; thrown into confusion
vocab.quote_en: "Mrs. Bennet was quite disconcerted. She could not imagine what business he could have in town so soon after his arrival in Hertfordshire."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特太太给弄得心乱如麻。她弄不明白，他才刚刚来到哈福德郡，怎么会有事要进城去。
vocab.ex_en: We are most disconcerted not by what we don't know, but by what we thought we knew and discover we did not.
vocab.ex_zh: 最让人不安的不是我们不知道的，而是我们以为自己知道却发现并非如此的东西。

vocab.def_en: Overindulgence in pleasure; wasteful expenditure; scattering
vocab.quote_en: "In town I believe he chiefly lived, but his studying the law was a mere pretense, and being now free from all restraint, his life was a life of idleness and dissipation."
vocab.quote_zh: 我相信他主要是住在城里，但是所谓学法律，不过是个借口罢了，现在他既然摆脱了一切束缚，便整天过着悠闲放荡的生活。
vocab.ex_en: The dissipation of youth is often the remorse of age.
vocab.ex_zh: 少年的放荡往往是老年的悔恨。

vocab.def_en: The ability to be drawn out or shaped; flexibility
vocab.quote_en: "Bingley was endeared to Darcy by the easiness, openness, ductility of his temper, though no disposition could offer a greater contrast to his own."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱所以得达西喜欢，就因为达西为人温柔敦厚、坦白直爽、性格柔顺，虽说这跟他自己的性格恰恰相反。
vocab.ex_en: The ductility of character determines whether we break under pressure or are shaped by it into something stronger.
vocab.ex_zh: 性格的韧性决定了我们是在压力下破碎，还是被塑造成更坚强的形态。

vocab.def_en: Deceitfulness; double-dealing
vocab.quote_en: "I cannot understand it. If I were not afraid of judging harshly, I should be almost tempted to say that there is a strong appearance of duplicity in all this."
vocab.quote_zh: 我真弄不明白。如果我不是怕说得过分，我几乎要说，这一切里面都有一种很明显的两面三刀的味道。
vocab.ex_en: Duplicity is the tribute that vice pays to virtue, pretending to be what it is not.
vocab.ex_zh: 口是心非是恶行向美德致敬的方式，假装成它不是的样子。

vocab.def_en: The ability to produce a desired or intended result
vocab.quote_en: "I wonder who first discovered the efficacy of poetry in driving away love!"
vocab.quote_zh: 我不知道是谁第一个发现了诗歌可以驱逐爱情的效能！
vocab.ex_en: The efficacy of our actions depends less on their force than on their wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们行动的功效，更多取决于其智慧而非力量。

vocab.def_en: An unrestrained expression of feelings in speech or writing
vocab.quote_en: "Her daughters listened in silence to this effusion, sensible that any attempt to reason with or sooth her would only increase the irritation."
vocab.quote_zh: 女儿们听着她这一番话，都不做声，她们知道，要是跟她讲理或是安慰她，反而会使她更烦躁。
vocab.ex_en: The effusion of genuine feeling touches hearts that carefully crafted words cannot reach.
vocab.ex_zh: 真挚情感的流露，能触动精心修饰的言语无法企及的心。

vocab.def_en: In high spirits; extremely happy and excited
vocab.def_zh: 兴高采烈的;得意洋洋的
vocab.quote_en: "For though elated by his rank, it did not render him supercilious; on the contrary, he was all attention to everybody."
vocab.quote_zh: 他虽然以自己的身份感到得意，可并没有因此而变得傲慢；相反，他对每个人都殷勤备至。
vocab.ex_en: Elation at success is sweetest when shared with those who rejoice in our joy.
vocab.ex_zh: 成功时的得意，与那些为我们的快乐而快乐的人分享时最为甜美。

vocab.def_en: An official ban on trade or other commercial activity; any restriction
vocab.quote_en: "She wanted to talk, but there seemed an embargo on every subject."
vocab.quote_zh: 她想说话，可是似乎每个话题都被封锁了。
vocab.ex_en: The heart places no embargo on love, though the tongue may be slow to declare it.
vocab.ex_zh: 心灵不为爱情设置障碍，尽管舌头可能迟迟不愿宣之于口。

vocab.def_en: Fame or acknowledged superiority within a particular sphere; high position
vocab.quote_en: "In describing to her all the grandeur of Lady Catherine and her mansion, with occasional digressions in praise of his own humble abode and the improvements it was receiving, he was happily employed."
vocab.quote_zh: 他得意非凡地告诉她咖苔琳夫人和她的府邸有多么伟大，又时常离题去赞美他自己的陋室和它的改善情况。
vocab.ex_en: Eminence achieved without integrity is but a gilded cage.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有正直伴随的显赫，不过是镀金的牢笼。

vocab.def_en: Expressing something forcibly and clearly
vocab.quote_en: "What can be the meaning of that emphatic exclamation?" cried he.
vocab.quote_zh: 他叫道：“你这种着重的感叹，究竟是什么意思？”
vocab.ex_en: An emphatic denial often reveals the truth it seeks to conceal.
vocab.ex_zh: 强调的否认常常暴露出它想要掩盖的真相。

vocab.def_en: An earnest or humble request
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, with some warmth, cried, 'You puzzle me exceedingly. If what I have hitherto said can appear to you in the form of encouragement, I know not how to express my refusal in such a way as may convince you of its being one.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白不禁有些生气，嚷道：“你真叫我太莫名其妙了。要是我先前说的话还会使你有意存奢望，那我真不知道怎么样拒绝你，才会使你死心塌地。”
vocab.ex_en: Entreaty from a sincere heart moves more than force from a mighty arm.
vocab.ex_zh: 来自真诚心灵的恳求，比强壮手臂的力量更能打动人。

vocab.def_en: A characterizing word or phrase accompanying or occurring in place of a name
vocab.quote_en: "As soon as they entered, Bingley looked at her so expresively, and shook hands with such warmth, as left no doubt of his good information; and he soon afterwards said aloud, 'Mrs. Bennet, have you no more lanes hereabouts in which Lizzy may lose her way again today?'"
vocab.quote_zh: 他们一走进来，彬格莱就意味深长地望着她，并且那么热烈地跟她握手，一望而知他确实得到了好消息；过了一会儿他大声说道：“班纳特太太，这附近一带还有别的曲径小道，可以让丽萃今天再去迷路吗？”
vocab.ex_en: An epithet carelessly spoken can wound more deeply than any reasoned argument.
vocab.ex_zh: 随口说出的称号，比任何有理有据的论据伤人更深。

vocab.def_en: Open to more than one interpretation; ambiguous
vocab.def_zh: 模棱两可的;含糊的;可疑的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth saw directly that her father had not the smallest intention of yielding; but his answers were at the same time so vague and equivocal, that her mother, though often disheartened, had never yet despaired of succeeding at last."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白立刻看出父亲根本没有让步的意思，可是他的回答却说得那么含含糊糊、模棱两可，使母亲虽然常常碰钉子，却从来没有死心塌地地失望过。
vocab.ex_en: The equivocal heart speaks in riddles, hoping to be understood without the risk of declaring itself.
vocab.ex_zh: 含糊其辞的心说话像谜语，希望被理解而又无需冒险表明自己。

vocab.def_en: An urgent need or demand
vocab.quote_en: "In such an exigence my uncle's advice and assistance would be everything in the world; he will immediately comprehend what I must feel, and I rely upon his goodness."
vocab.quote_zh: 在这样紧急的时候，姨爹的指教和帮助就显得无比重要；他会马上体会到我的心情，我相信他一定肯帮助我们的。
vocab.ex_en: In moments of exigence, true character reveals itself without the mask of daily convention.
vocab.ex_zh: 在紧急关头，真正的性格卸下日常惯例的面具显现出来。

vocab.def_en: With speed and efficiency
vocab.quote_en: "They traveled as expeditiously as possible; and, sleeping one night on the road, reached Longbourn by dinner-time the next day."
vocab.quote_zh: 她们尽可能赶路快些，在路上歇了一夜，第二天吃饭的时候就到了浪搏恩。
vocab.ex_en: That which is done expeditiously is not always done well, but that which is done well requires its own proper time.
vocab.ex_zh: 做得快的不一定做得好，但要做得好也需要适当的时间。

vocab.def_en: Lessening the seriousness of an offense by partial excuse
vocab.def_zh: 情有可原的;减轻罪责的
vocab.quote_en: "Miss Bennet was the only creature who could suppose there might be any extenuating circumstances in the case unknown to the society of Hertfordshire."
vocab.quote_zh: 只有班纳特小姐一个人认为，这件事也许还有些情有可原，为哈福德郡的其他人所不知道。
vocab.ex_en: There are extenuating circumstances in every story we have not fully heard.
vocab.ex_zh: 每一个我们没有完全听到的故事里，都有情有可原的情况。

vocab.def_en: To obtain something by force, threats, or undue pressure
vocab.quote_en: "She did at last extort from her father an acknowledgment that the horses were engaged."
vocab.quote_zh: 她最后总算逼得父亲不得不承认，家里的马已经有别的用处了。
vocab.ex_en: We cannot extort love; it must be freely given to be truly received.
vocab.ex_zh: 爱无法强求；它必须自由给予才能真正被接受。

vocab.def_en: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; difficult to please
vocab.def_zh: 挑剔的;苛求的;过分讲究的
vocab.quote_en: "'I would not be so fastidious as you are,' cried Bingley, 'for a kingdom!'"
vocab.quote_zh: “你就是给我一个王国，我也不愿像你这样挑剔，”彬格莱嚷道。
vocab.ex_en: The fastidious critic of others often proves most lenient when judging his own failings.
vocab.ex_zh: 对别人挑剔的人，评判自己的缺点时往往最为宽容。

vocab.def_en: To lose or be deprived of as a penalty for wrongdoing
vocab.quote_en: "My father's opinion of me does me the greatest honor; and I should be miserable to forfeit it."
vocab.quote_zh: 父亲对我的器重，实在是我最大的荣幸，我要是失去了它，一定会感到难受。
vocab.ex_en: What we gain by deception we forfeit in self-respect, and the exchange is never equal.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们通过欺骗获得的东西，丧失的是自尊，这笔交易从不公平。

vocab.def_en: Characterized by exaggerated or affected politeness, refinement, and respectability
vocab.def_zh: 上流社会的;彬彬有礼的;文雅的
vocab.quote_en: "By Mrs. Hurst and Miss Bingley, they were noticed only by a curtsy; and on their being seated, a pause, awkward as such pauses must always be, succeeded for a few moments. It was first broken by Mrs. Annesley, a genteel, agreeable-looking woman."
vocab.quote_zh: 赫斯脱太太和彬格莱小姐只对她们行了个屈膝礼。她们坐下以后，沉默了好一会儿，这种沉默照例是多么 awkward。第一个开口的是安斯莱太太，她是个和蔼可亲的漂亮女人。
vocab.ex_en: True gentility is not in manners but in the heart that makes manners meaningful.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正的文雅不在于举止，而在于使举止有意义的内心。

vocab.def_en: Utterly odious or wicked
vocab.def_zh: 可憎的;十恶不赦的;令人发指的
vocab.quote_en: "Let me advise you then, my dear Sir, to console yourself as much as possible, to throw off your unworthy child from your affection forever, and leave her to reap the fruits of her own heinous offense."
vocab.quote_zh: 那么，我最亲爱的先生，我劝你尽可能地去安慰自己，把那个不孝的女儿永远赶出你心坎，让她去自食其果吧。
vocab.ex_en: The most heinous crimes begin not with monstrous intent but with small surrenders of conscience.
vocab.ex_zh: 最可憎的罪行并非始于骇人的意图，而是始于良知的微小让步。

vocab.def_en: A secluded residence or retreat; the dwelling of a hermit
vocab.def_zh: 隐居处;修道院;僻静的住所
vocab.quote_en: "'Go, my dear,' cried her mother, 'and show her ladyship about the different walks. I think she will be pleased with the hermitage.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “去吧，乖孩子，”她妈妈叫道，“带夫人到各条小径上去走走。我相信她一定会喜欢那个清静的去处。”
vocab.ex_en: The soul needs its hermitage, a place apart where the noise of the world cannot reach.
vocab.ex_zh: 灵魂需要它的隐修处，一个世界喧嚣无法到达的僻静之所。

vocab.def_en: To drive, force, or urge someone to do something
vocab.quote_en: "I cannot blame myself for having done thus much. There is but one part of my conduct in the whole affair on which I do not reflect with satisfaction; it is that I condescended to adopt the measures of art so far as to conceal from him your sister's being in town."
vocab.quote_zh: 这件事我只能做到这样，我不能责备自己。对于整个这件事，只有一点我回想起来不觉得满意，那就是，我竟然那么下贱，居然不择手段到了这样的地步，竟瞒住了他，不让他知道你姐姐来到了城里。
vocab.ex_en: Love impels us to heights we never knew we could reach, and depths we never knew we could survive.
vocab.ex_zh: 爱驱使我们去到从未想到能到达的高度，也经历从未想到能承受的深度。

vocab.def_en: Not showing proper respect; rude; irrelevant
vocab.def_zh: 无礼的;粗鲁的;不相干的
vocab.quote_en: "I will take care of myself, and of Mr. Wickham too. He shall not be in love with me, if I can prevent it."
vocab.quote_zh: “我会照顾我自己，也会照顾韦翰先生。要是我能阻止他爱我，我一定不让他爱。”
vocab.ex_en: The impertinent question often reveals more about the asker than the answer ever could about the subject.
vocab.ex_zh: 无礼的问题往往暴露提问者，甚于答案揭示真相。

vocab.def_en: Acting or done quickly and without thought or care
vocab.quote_en: "Her pale face and impetuous manner made him start, and before he could recover himself enough to speak, she, in whose mind every idea was superseded by Lydia's situation, hastily exclaimed, 'I beg your pardon, but I must leave you.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 只见她脸色苍白，神态仓皇，他不由得吃了一惊。他还没有定下心来说一句话，她却已经迫不及待地嚷道：“请原谅，我得马上离开你，我得马上去找嘉丁纳先生。”
vocab.ex_en: The impetuous heart leaps before it looks, and often falls before it flies.
vocab.ex_zh: 冲动的心里飞跃前不看，往往未飞先坠。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being unable to be appeased or pacified
vocab.def_zh: 难以平息;不可调和;执拗
vocab.quote_en: "I do remember his boasting one day, at Netherfield, of the implacability of his resentments, of his having an unforgiving temper."
vocab.quote_zh: 我记得他有一次在尼日斐花园曾经自高自大地谈起过，如果他一旦对人有了成见，那就永远无法消除，他的心肠真硬。
vocab.ex_en: The implacability of our judgments against others is often the measure of our unforgiveness toward ourselves.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们对他人的不可调和，往往是我们对自己的不可原谅的尺度。

vocab.def_en: Unable to be placated; relentless; unstoppable
vocab.def_zh: 无法平息的;毫不宽容的;坚定不移的
vocab.quote_en: "'That is a failing indeed!'—cried Elizabeth. 'Implacable resentment is a shade in a character. But you have chosen your fault well.—I really cannot laugh at it. You are safe from me.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “那的确是一种缺点！”伊丽莎白叫道。“怨恨的无法消除，的确是性格上的一个阴影。不过你选择你的缺点选得好——我真不能笑你。你是安全的，我不会笑你。”
vocab.ex_en: The implacable heart builds walls so high it cannot see over them to the love that waits outside.
vocab.ex_zh: 执拗的心筑起高墙，高得它无法望见墙外等待的爱。

vocab.def_en: Implied though not directly expressed; absolute, unquestioning
vocab.quote_en: "Let me recommend you, however, as a friend, not to give implicit confidence to all his assertions; for as to Mr. Darcy's using him ill, it is perfectly false; for, on the contrary, he has always been remarkably kind to him."
vocab.quote_zh: 可是我劝你，作为一个朋友，不要盲目轻信他的话。达西先生待他不好，完全是捏造出来的；相反地，达西先生一直待他非常好。
vocab.ex_en: The greatest truths require not implicit faith but examined understanding.
vocab.ex_zh: 最伟大的真理不需要盲目信仰，而需要经过检验的理解。

vocab.def_en: Unwise; not expedient
vocab.def_zh: 失策的;不明智的;不审慎的
vocab.quote_en: "'Indeed, Mr. Darcy, it is very ungenerous in you to mention all that you knew to my disadvantage in Hertfordshire—and, give me leave to say, very impolitic too—for it is provoking me to retaliate.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “达西先生，你把我在哈福德郡的一些事都说了出来，这未免有失厚道，而且也很不策略——因为这是在挑动我报复。”
vocab.ex_en: The impolitic truth often serves better than the politic lie, though both may cost us dearly.
vocab.ex_zh: 失策的真相往往比圆滑的谎言更有益，尽管两者都可能让我们付出沉重代价。

vocab.def_en: To harass with persistent requests
vocab.quote_en: "I will no longer importune my young cousin."
vocab.quote_zh: 我再也不麻烦我的年轻表妹了。
vocab.ex_en: To importune another for what they cannot freely give is to seek a gift that loses its value in the taking.
vocab.ex_zh: 强求他人不能自由给予的东西，是在追求一份因索取而失去价值的礼物。

vocab.def_en: Lack of caution; unwise behavior
vocab.quote_en: "My dear aunt, this is being serious indeed."
vocab.quote_zh: 好舅妈，你这话说得真是郑重其事。
vocab.ex_en: Youth's imprudence is age's lesson, taught too late to benefit the learner.
vocab.ex_zh: 少年的轻率是暮年的教训，学到时已太晚，无法让学习者受益。

vocab.def_en: Exemption from punishment or freedom from the injurious consequences of an action
vocab.quote_en: "'But surely,' said she, 'I may enter his county with impunity, and rob it of a few petrified spars without his perceiving me.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 她自言自语道：“可是我进了他的郡，偷他几块宝石，他也不会知道。”
vocab.ex_en: No one acts with true impunity, for every action writes itself upon the soul.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有人能真正免于后果，因为每个行为都写在灵魂上。

vocab.def_en: To attribute something, especially a fault or crime, to someone
vocab.quote_en: "Breakfast was scarcely over when a servant from Netherfield brought the following note for Elizabeth: 'My dearest Lizzy, I find myself very unwell this morning, which, I suppose, is to be imputed to my getting wet through yesterday.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 早饭还没吃完，尼日斐花园就打发了人送来一封信给伊丽莎白。信上写着：“我最亲爱的丽萃，我今天早上觉得很不舒服，我想这可能是昨天淋了雨的缘故。”
vocab.ex_en: We impute to others the motives we would have in their place, forgetting they may not be like us.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们把自己设身处地的动机归咎于他人，忘了他们可能与我们不同。

vocab.def_en: Continuing without pause or interruption
vocab.def_zh: 持续不断的;不停的;无间断的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, who knew that her mother owed to the latter the preservation of her favorite daughter from irremediable infamy, was hurt and distressed to a most painful degree by a distinction so ill applied."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白知道，她心爱的女儿所以能够免于身败名裂，全靠了达西先生，因此对她母亲这种过于苛待他的态度，感到非常痛苦。
vocab.ex_en: The incessant chatter of the mind drowns out the still, small voice of wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 头脑持续不断的唠叨淹没了智慧那微小而安静的声音。

vocab.def_en: The state of being unwilling or unable to believe something
vocab.quote_en: "She did indeed! To be sure, he had not written with such vehemence as to make her suppose he had any design of obtaining her hand."
vocab.quote_zh: 她确实这样！当然，他的信写得并不十分热烈，决不会使她猜想他有向她求婚的打算。
vocab.ex_en: Incredulity is the mind's shield against the too wonderful, too terrible, too improbable.
vocab.ex_zh: 怀疑是心灵的盾牌，抵御过于美妙、过于可怕、过于不可能的事物。

vocab.def_en: Necessary for someone as a duty or responsibility
vocab.def_zh: 有责任的;义不容辞的;现任的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, feeling it incumbent on her to relieve him from so unpleasant a situation, now put herself forward to confirm his account, by mentioning her prior knowledge of it from Charlotte herself."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白觉得自己有责任帮他摆脱这种不愉快的局面，于是挺身而出，证明他的话说得对，提到她事先从夏绿蒂那儿知道了这件事。
vocab.ex_en: It is incumbent upon each generation to pass on not only knowledge but the wisdom to use it well.
vocab.ex_zh: 每一代都有责任传递的不仅是知识，还有善用知识的智慧。

vocab.def_en: Slightly unwell; averse or unwilling
vocab.def_zh: 身体不适的;不愿意的;不情愿的
vocab.quote_en: "Maria thought speaking out of the question, and the gentlemen did nothing but eat and admire."
vocab.quote_zh: 曼丽觉得不便说话，男宾们只顾一边吃一边赞美。
vocab.ex_en: The heart indisposed to love will find a thousand reasons to withhold what it fears to give.
vocab.ex_zh: 不愿爱的心会找到千万个理由，拒绝给予它害怕给予的东西。

vocab.def_en: Avoidance of activity or exertion; laziness
vocab.quote_en: "When the first transports of rage which had produced his activity in seeking her were over, he naturally returned to all his former indolence."
vocab.quote_zh: 当他当初的一阵愤怒，那股促使他四处寻找女儿的冲动过去以后，他又自然而然地回复到从前那种懒洋洋的状态中去了。
vocab.ex_en: Indolence is the grave of possibility, burying what might have been under what merely is.
vocab.ex_zh: 懒惰是可能的坟墓，将本可能如何埋葬在本该如何之下。

vocab.def_en: Wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy
vocab.def_zh: 懒惰的;怠惰的;不活跃的
vocab.quote_en: "She had very little notice from any but him. Miss Bingley was engrossed by Mr. Darcy, her sister scarcely less so; and as for Mr. Hurst, by whom Elizabeth sat, he was an indolent man, who lived only to eat, drink, and play at cards."
vocab.quote_zh: 除了他以外，没有人理睬伊丽莎白。彬格莱小姐一心一意在达西先生身上打转，她姐姐也差不多；至于赫斯脱先生，他就坐在伊丽莎白身旁，他生性懒惰，活在世上就是为了吃喝玩乐。
vocab.ex_en: An indolent mind is fertile ground for the weeds of prejudice to take root.
vocab.ex_zh: 懒散的心智是偏见这株杂草生根发芽的沃土。

vocab.def_en: A thing that persuades or influences someone to do something
vocab.quote_en: "There is so much of gratitude or vanity in almost every attachment, that it is not safe to leave any to itself."
vocab.quote_zh: 几乎所有的恋爱里都有那么多的感激或虚荣心，所以听其自然是最不保险的。
vocab.ex_en: The strongest inducement to virtue is not fear of punishment but love of the good itself.
vocab.ex_zh: 对美德最强的诱因不是对惩罚的恐惧，而是对善本身的爱。

vocab.def_en: The state of being well known for some bad quality or deed
vocab.def_zh: 声名狼藉;臭名昭著;丑名
vocab.quote_en: "She was sensible that no one liked him but Jane; and even feared that with the others it was a dislike which not all his fortune and consequence might do away."
vocab.quote_zh: 她感到除了吉英以外，家里没有一个人喜欢他，她甚至担心家里人对他都怀着一种厌恶感，那可能是他的财产和社会地位所不能消除的。
vocab.ex_en: Infamy is fame's dark twin, born of deeds we wish undone.
vocab.ex_zh: 臭名昭著是名声的黑暗双生，生于我们希望从未做过的行为。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being clever, original, and inventive
vocab.def_zh: 聪明才智;独创性;心灵手巧
vocab.quote_en: "but I dare say it would succeed, if Miss de Bourgh were out of the way."
vocab.quote_zh: 我想，要是德・包尔小姐挡不住路，她一定会成功。
vocab.ex_en: Necessity is the mother of ingenuity, but patience is its father.
vocab.ex_zh: 需要是创造之母，但耐心是创造之父。

vocab.def_en: An authoritative warning or order
vocab.quote_en: "Lizzy, I insist upon your staying and hearing Mr. Collins."
vocab.quote_zh: “丽萃，我非要你待在这儿听柯林斯先生讲不可。”
vocab.ex_en: The heart responds to persuasion where it would rebel against injunction.
vocab.ex_zh: 心对劝说有回应，而对命令会反抗。

vocab.def_en: Suggesting or hinting in an indirect and unpleasant way
vocab.def_zh: 暗示的;含沙射影的;曲意逢迎的
vocab.quote_en: "That he has neither integrity nor honor. That he is as false and deceitful as he is insinuating."
vocab.quote_zh: 他没有道德观念，没有荣誉感。他真是虚伪透顶，一面花言巧语，一面 himself假仁假义。
vocab.ex_en: The insinuating whisper does more damage than the shouted accusation, for it enters where defenses are down.
vocab.ex_zh: 含沙射影的低语比大声的指责伤害更大，因为它趁防御松懈时进入。

vocab.def_en: Lacking flavor or interest; dull
vocab.def_zh: 乏味的;枯燥的;无生气的
vocab.quote_en: "I was never more annoyed! The insipidity and yet the noise; the nothingness and yet the self-importance of all these people!"
vocab.quote_zh: 我从来没有这样烦过！这些人既乏味又吵闹，既无聊又自以为是！
vocab.ex_en: Better to be controversial than insipid, for at least controversy stirs thought.
vocab.ex_zh: 宁可有争议也不可乏味，因为至少争议能激发思考。

vocab.def_en: Lack of flavor or interest; dullness
vocab.quote_en: "The insipidity of the meeting was exactly such as Elizabeth had expected."
vocab.quote_zh: 这次宴会的平淡无奇，正合伊丽莎白的预料。
vocab.ex_en: The soul cannot thrive on the insipidity of mere existence; it hungers for meaning.
vocab.ex_zh: 灵魂无法仅靠存在的平淡生存；它渴求意义。

vocab.def_en: Showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect
vocab.quote_en: "she could not determine whether the silent contempt of the gentleman, or the insolent smiles of the ladies, were more intolerable."
vocab.quote_zh: 她分辨不出究竟是那位先生的无声轻蔑，还是两位女士的傲慢笑容更让人难以忍受。
vocab.ex_en: Insolence is often the mask that insecurity wears when it ventures into company.
vocab.ex_zh: 傲慢往往是缺乏安全感的人走入人群时所戴的面具。

vocab.def_en: Unable to be endured; unbearable
vocab.def_zh: 无法忍受的;难以承受的
vocab.quote_en: "At such an assembly as this, it would be insupportable. Your sisters are engaged, and there is not another woman in the room whom it would not be a punishment to me to stand up with."
vocab.quote_zh: 在这种舞会上跳舞，简直叫人受不了。你的姐妹们都在跟别人跳，叫舞场里别的女人跟我跳，那对我简直就是受罪。
vocab.ex_en: Pride makes the smallest slight insupportable and the greatest kindness invisible.
vocab.ex_zh: 骄傲使最小的轻蔑无法忍受，使最大的善意视而不见。

vocab.def_en: To accustom someone to something, especially something unpleasant
vocab.quote_en: "I speak feelingly. A younger son, you know, must be inured to self-denial and dependence."
vocab.quote_zh: 我说这话是深有体会的。你知道，小儿子就得习惯于克制自己，仰仗别人。
vocab.ex_en: We inure ourselves to discomfort only to discover we have lost the capacity for delight.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们让自己习惯于不适，却发现自己失去了快乐的能力。

vocab.def_en: Tiresome; annoying
vocab.def_zh: 令人厌烦的;讨厌的;烦人的
vocab.quote_en: "Mr. Collins to be sure was neither sensible nor agreeable; his society was irksome, and his attachment to her must be imaginary."
vocab.quote_zh: 柯林斯先生固然既不通情达理，又不讨人喜欢，和他相处实在令人厌烦，他对她的爱也一定是空中楼阁。
vocab.ex_en: The irksome duties of today become the cherished memories of tomorrow.
vocab.ex_zh: 今日令人厌烦的责任，成为明日珍贵的记忆。

vocab.def_en: Impossible to cure or put right
vocab.def_zh: 无法治愈的;不可救药的;无法补救的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth particularly, who knew that her mother owed to the latter the preservation of her favorite daughter from irremediable infamy, was hurt and distressed to a most painful degree by a distinction so ill applied."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白知道，她心爱的女儿所以能够免于身败名裂，全靠了达西先生，因此对她母亲这种过于苛待他的态度，感到非常痛苦。
vocab.ex_en: Some wounds are irremediable not because they cannot heal, but because we will not let them.
vocab.ex_zh: 有些伤痛无法治愈，不是因为它们不能愈合，而是因为我们不让它们愈合。

vocab.def_en: Beyond criticism; faultless
vocab.def_zh: 无可指责的;无懈可击的;完美的
vocab.quote_en: "'The conduct of neither, if strictly examined, will be irreproachable; but since then, we have both, I hope, improved in civility.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 要是严格地检查起来，双方的行为都不见无可非议；但是从那次以后，我们双方都学会了礼貌，我希望。
vocab.ex_en: The irreproachable life is not one without error, but one that learns from every error.
vocab.ex_zh: 无可指责的生活不是没有错误，而是从每个错误中学习。

vocab.def_en: In a way that cannot be changed, reversed, or recovered
vocab.def_zh: 不可改变地;无法挽回地;最后地
vocab.quote_en: "'I knew enough of your disposition to be certain, that, had you been absolutely, irrevocably decided against me, you would have acknowledged it to Lady Catherine, frankly and openly.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “我对你的性情知道得很够，我断定，要是你坚决地、断然地决定反对我，你一定会到咖苔琳夫人面前去，老老实实地说出来。”
vocab.ex_en: The irrevocably said cannot be unsaid; the irrevocably done cannot be undone.
vocab.ex_zh: 已说出口的无法收回；已做成的事无法撤销。

vocab.def_en: To reject or cast off (a lover) after encouragement
vocab.quote_en: "Let Wickham be your man. He is a pleasant fellow, and would jilt you creditably."
vocab.quote_zh: 让韦翰做你的对象吧。他是个有趣的家伙，他会用很体面的方式把你甩掉。
vocab.ex_en: Those who jilt others imagine they escape unscathed, but the character they betray is their own.
vocab.ex_zh: 抛弃他人者以为自己能全身而退，但他们背叛的是自己的品格。

vocab.def_en: Using very few words; terse
vocab.quote_en: "But their father, though very laconic in his expressions of pleasure, was really glad to see them."
vocab.quote_zh: 可是她们的爸爸，虽然看到她们回来很高兴，却只是简简单单地说了几句。
vocab.ex_en: The laconic speaker often conveys more in silence than the verbose in volumes.
vocab.ex_zh: 寡言者常常在沉默中传达出比冗长者千言万语更多的东西。

vocab.def_en: Lack of energy; listlessness; a state of dreamy inactivity
vocab.quote_en: "They repulsed every attempt of Mrs. Bennet at conversation, and by so doing threw a languor over the whole party, which was very little relieved by the long speeches of Mr. Collins."
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特太太每想攀谈，都给他们顶了回去，这样一来，弄得大家都很没趣，柯林斯先生冗长的言论也解不了围。
vocab.ex_en: The languor of a summer afternoon invites reflection, where the rush of daily life forbids it.
vocab.ex_zh: 夏日午后的慵懒引人沉思，而日常生活的匆忙则禁止沉思。

vocab.def_en: Lack of moral restraint, especially in sexual matters
vocab.quote_en: "And it is the more to be lamented, because there is reason to suppose, as my dear Charlotte informs me, that this licentiousness of behavior in your daughter has proceeded from a faulty degree of indulgence."
vocab.quote_zh: 这件事尤其使人痛心，因为据我的亲爱的夏绿蒂说，你女儿这种放荡不羁的行为，完全是出于溺爱娇惯。
vocab.ex_en: Licentiousness mistakes freedom for license, not knowing that true liberty is found within the bounds of virtue.
vocab.ex_zh: 放荡将自由误认为放纵，不知道真正的自由存在于美德的边界之内。

vocab.def_en: Motivated primarily by a desire for money or material gain
vocab.def_zh: 唯利是图的;贪财的;雇佣的
vocab.quote_en: "'Pray, my dear aunt, what is the difference in matrimonial affairs, between the mercenary and the prudent motive? Where does discretion end, and avarice begin?'"
vocab.quote_zh: “请问，好舅妈，在婚姻问题上，唯利是图和谨慎行事有什么不同？审慎在何处结束，贪婪在何处开始？”
vocab.ex_en: The truly prudent consider not only what they gain, but what they become in the gaining.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正谨慎的人不仅考虑他们得到什么，还考虑在得到的过程中他们变成了什么。

vocab.def_en: A person's look or manner, especially one of a particular kind indicating their character or mood
vocab.quote_en: "The gentlemen pronounced him to be a fine figure of a man, the ladies declared he was much handsomer than Mr. Bingley, and he was looked at with great admiration for about half the evening, till his manners gave a disgust which turned the tide of his popularity; for he was discovered to be proud, to be above his company, and above being pleased; and not all his large estate in Derbyshire could then save him from having a most forbidding, disagreeable countenance."
vocab.quote_zh: 男宾们都称赞他仪表堂堂，女宾们都说他比彬格莱先生漂亮得多，人们差不多有半个晚上都带着爱慕的目光望着他。最后，他的举止引起了大家的厌恶，他在人们的心目中也就一落千丈，因为大家发觉他骄傲自大，高不可攀，不愿凑热闹；纵使他在德比郡的财产再多，也无济于事，他那副面孔总是那样讨人嫌，那样惹人厌。
vocab.ex_en: A noble mien without a noble heart is but a beautiful mask over emptiness.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有高贵心灵的崇高风采，不过是覆盖着空虚的美丽面具。

vocab.def_en: To cause to feel embarrassed, ashamed, or humiliated
vocab.quote_en: "'If I can but see one of my daughters happily settled at Netherfield,' said Mrs. Bennet, 'and all the others equally well married, I shall have nothing to wish for.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 班纳特太太说：“只要能看到一个女儿在尼日斐花园幸福地安了家，其他几个也嫁得一样好，我就再也没什么可奢望的了。”
vocab.ex_en: We mortify ourselves daily through expectations that exceed reality and regrets that cannot change it.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们每日通过超过现实的期望和无法改变现实的后悔而自寻烦恼。

vocab.def_en: Failing to take proper care; habitually careless
vocab.def_zh: 疏忽的;粗心大意的;玩忽职守的
vocab.quote_en: "His family knew him to be, on all common occasions, a most negligent and dilatory correspondent, but at such a time, they had hoped for exertion."
vocab.quote_zh: 家里人都知道，他平常总是个最懒得动笔的人，可是这一次她们却希望他能勉为其难。
vocab.ex_en: The negligent heart imagines there will always be time tomorrow for what it neglects today.
vocab.ex_zh: 疏忽的心以为今天忽视的事，明天总会有时间去做。

vocab.def_en: Irritated or annoyed
vocab.quote_en: "Persuaded as Miss Bingley was that Darcy admired Elizabeth, this was not the best method of recommending herself; but angry people are not always wise; and in seeing him at last look somewhat nettled, she had all the success she expected."
vocab.quote_zh: 彬格莱小姐既然一味想要达西爱上伊丽莎白，这当然不是推荐自己的最好办法；但是恼恨的人往往不聪明；她看到他终于有些生气的样子，那股得意劲儿也就完全达到了目的。
vocab.ex_en: The nettled heart speaks words it later regrets, while the composed mind chooses silence.
vocab.ex_zh: 被激怒的心说出日后后悔的话，而沉着的心选择沉默。

vocab.def_en: Deferential respect; a gesture of respect such as a bow or curtsy
vocab.quote_en: "The two gentlemen left Rosings the next morning; and Mr. Collins, having been in waiting near the lodges to make them his parting obeisance, was able to bring home the pleasing intelligence of their appearing in very good health."
vocab.quote_zh: 两位先生第二天早上就离开了罗新斯；柯林斯先生在门房附近等候着，向他们告别辞行，然后带回家去一个愉快的消息，说他们两位虽然刚刚离开了罗新斯，身体却很好。
vocab.ex_en: Obeisance paid to rank alone is empty ceremony; obeisance paid to worth is the heart's true homage.
vocab.ex_zh: 只对地位表示的敬礼是空洞的仪式；对价值表示的敬礼才是心灵的真正敬意。

vocab.def_en: Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree
vocab.def_zh: 谄媚的;奉承的;卑躬屈膝的
vocab.quote_en: "made him altogether a mixture of pride and obsequiousness, self-importance and humility."
vocab.quote_zh: 使他变得傲慢自大、又卑躬屈膝、自高自大、又虚心下气。
vocab.ex_en: The obsequious soul bends so low it can no longer see its own reflection.
vocab.ex_zh: 谄媚的灵魂弯得如此之低，再也看不见自己的倒影。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being excessively obedient or attentive
vocab.quote_en: "made him altogether a mixture of pride and obsequiousness, self-importance and humility."
vocab.quote_zh: 使他变得傲慢自大、又卑躬屈膝、自高自大、又虚心下气。
vocab.ex_en: Obsequiousness is the mirror in which vanity sees itself most clearly.
vocab.ex_zh: 谄媚是虚荣看清自己的镜子。

vocab.def_en: To become noticeable in an unwelcome or intrusive way
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth longed to explore its windings; but when they had crossed the bridge, and perceived their distance from the house, Mrs. Gardiner, who was not a great walker, could go no farther."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白想去探幽寻胜，可是她们一过了桥，只见离住宅那么远，嘉丁纳太太走不了远路，只得作罢。
vocab.ex_en: Thoughts obtrude themselves upon us unbidden, but we choose which to entertain.
vocab.ex_zh: 思想不请自来地闯入我们心中，但我们选择款待哪些。

vocab.def_en: Extremely unpleasant; repulsive
vocab.quote_en: "'Oh! my dear,' cried his wife, 'I cannot bear to hear that mentioned. Pray do not talk of that odious man.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “哦！我的好老爷，”太太叫道，“一提到这件事，我就受不了。请你别谈那个讨厌的家伙吧。”
vocab.ex_en: We call others odious for faults we cannot bear to see in ourselves.
vocab.ex_zh: 我们把别人称为可憎，是因为他们身上有我们无法忍受在自己身上看到的缺点。

vocab.def_en: Assertive of authority in a domineering way, especially with regard to trivial matters
vocab.def_zh: 多管闲事的;好发号施令的;爱指手画脚的
vocab.quote_en: "'You are rather disposed to call his interference officious?'"
vocab.quote_zh: “你未免太喜欢指责他的行为是多管闲事吧？”
vocab.ex_en: The officious heart imagines it helps while only hindering, serving not others but its own need to serve.
vocab.ex_zh: 好管闲事的心以为自己在帮忙，实则只是阻碍，它服务的不是他人，而是自己服务的需要。

vocab.def_en: The pretentious or showy display of wealth or knowledge
vocab.quote_en: "Affectation of candor is common enough;—one meets it everywhere. But to be candid without ostentation or design—to take the good of everybody's character and make it still better, and say nothing of the bad—belongs to you alone."
vocab.quote_zh: 装模作样的坦率是再普通不过的事——到处都能碰到。可是，不摆架子、不存心机地坦率——只看到每个人的优点，把它说得更好，对缺点绝口不提——这只有你才做得到。
vocab.ex_en: Ostentation seeks admiration but earns only the contempt of discerning eyes.
vocab.ex_zh: 炫耀寻求赞美，却只赢得洞察者的轻蔑。

vocab.def_en: The action of lessening the severity of something without curing it; mitigation
vocab.quote_en: "It was, on the contrary, exactly calculated to make her understand her own wishes; and never had she so honestly felt that she could have loved him, as now, when all love must be vain."
vocab.quote_zh: 恰恰相反，这倒使她越发了解自己的心愿，她从来也没有象现在这样深切地体会到，她是多么爱他，可是现在已经无法相爱了。
vocab.ex_en: Palliation of symptoms without addressing causes is the medicine of fools.
vocab.ex_zh: 只减轻症状而不解决病因，是愚者的医术。

vocab.def_en: Relating to or consisting of money
vocab.quote_en: "He had some intention, he added, of studying the law, and I must be aware that the interest of one thousand pounds would be a very insufficient support therein."
vocab.quote_zh: 他又说，他有意学法律，他应该知道，一千磅的利息无论如何也不够他用来学法律。
vocab.ex_en: Pecuniary considerations alone cannot sustain a marriage, though their absence can destroy one.
vocab.ex_zh: 仅有金钱的考虑不能维持婚姻，但金钱的缺乏可以毁掉婚姻。

vocab.def_en: Excessively concerned with minor details or rules; overscrupulous
vocab.def_zh: 迂腐的;学究式的;卖弄学问的
vocab.quote_en: "Mary had neither genius nor taste; and though vanity had given her application, it had given her likewise a pedantic air and conceited manner, which would have injured a higher degree of excellence than she had reached."
vocab.quote_zh: 曼丽既没有天才，也没有情趣；虽说虚荣心促使她勤学苦练，但也造成了她一本正经的气派和自高自大的态度，有了这种气派和态度，即使她的造诣再高，也会受到损害。
vocab.ex_en: The pedantic mind mistakes knowledge of details for wisdom, not knowing that wisdom sees the whole.
vocab.ex_zh: 迂腐的心把对细节的了解误认为智慧，不知道智慧看到的是整体。

vocab.def_en: Easily irritated, especially by unimportant things
vocab.def_zh: 易怒的;脾气坏的;急躁的
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth had only to suffer from the uncomfortable feelings necessarily attending it, and occasionally from some peevish allusion of her mother."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白只感到心里不痛快，对于这件事感到很别扭，此外还要常常遭到她母亲絮聒的埋怨。
vocab.ex_en: The peevish soul blames the world for its own internal weather.
vocab.ex_zh: 脾气坏的人把自己的内心天气归咎于世界。

vocab.def_en: The ability to understand things quickly and clearly; insight
vocab.quote_en: "Her father had most cruelly mortified her by what he said of Mr. Darcy's indifference, and she could do nothing but wonder at such a want of penetration, or fear that perhaps, instead of his seeing too little, she might have fancied too much."
vocab.quote_zh: 父亲说到达西先生对他漠不关心，这话大大地伤了他的心，她只有怪父亲为什么这样没有眼光，同时又担心，也许是自己在胡思乱想。
vocab.ex_en: Penetration without charity sees truth but cannot love it; with charity, it sees and heals.
vocab.ex_zh: 没有仁爱的洞察力看见真理却不能爱它；有了仁爱，它看见并治愈。

vocab.def_en: Feeling or showing sorrow and regret for having done wrong
vocab.quote_en: "His style was not penitent, but haughty. It was all pride and insolence."
vocab.quote_zh: 他的措辞不是忏悔，而是傲慢。完全是傲慢无礼。
vocab.ex_en: The truly penitent heart seeks not to excuse but to amend, not to explain but to transform.
vocab.ex_zh: 真正忏悔的心寻求的不是开脱而是改正，不是解释而是转变。

vocab.def_en: To continue in a course of action even in the face of difficulty
vocab.def_zh: 坚持;孜孜以求;不屈不挠
vocab.quote_en: "On Miss Lucas's persevering, however, she added, 'Very well; if it must be so, it must.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 卢卡斯小姐再三要求，她只得又说：“好吧，既是非献丑不可，那就献丑吧。”
vocab.ex_en: Those who persevere not in stubbornness but in truth find that truth itself sustains them.
vocab.ex_zh: 那些不是固执而是坚持真理的人，会发现真理本身支撑着他们。

vocab.def_en: Anxiety; mental uneasiness
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, as they drove along, watched for the first appearance of Pemberley Woods with some perturbation; and when at length they turned in at the lodge, her spirits were in a high flutter."
vocab.quote_zh: 他们一路驶去，伊丽莎白心神不安地注视着彭伯里树林的出现；当终于看到门房时，她心里七上八下。
vocab.ex_en: Perturbation of spirit is the soul's alarm, warning that something within requires attention.
vocab.ex_zh: 精神的不安是灵魂的警报，警告内心有些东西需要注意。

vocab.def_en: Featuring or showing anxiety; unsettled
vocab.quote_en: "In this perturbed state of mind, with thoughts that could rest on nothing, she walked on; but it would not do; in half a minute the letter was unfolded again."
vocab.quote_zh: 她心乱如麻地往前走，实在定不下心来；不到半分钟，又把信摊开。
vocab.ex_en: The perturbed mind seeks resolution but finds only more questions until it learns to rest in uncertainty.
vocab.ex_zh: 心乱的心寻求解决，却只找到更多问题，直到它学会在不确定中安息。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being obstinately or unreasonably contrary
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth could not be prevailed on to judge it as improbable."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白无法被说服，认为这不太可能。
vocab.ex_en: Perverseness of spirit delights in opposition for its own sake, mistaking resistance for integrity.
vocab.ex_zh: 精神的执拗以对立本身为乐，误将反抗当作正直。

vocab.def_en: A feeling of irritation or resentment resulting from a slight, especially to one's pride
vocab.quote_en: "'Pride,' observed Mary, who piqued herself upon the solidity of her reflections, 'is a very common failing I believe.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 曼丽说：“虚荣和骄傲是两回事，虽然这两个词常常被混用。一个人可以骄傲而不虚荣。骄傲多半涉及我们对自己的看法，虚荣则涉及我们希望别人对我们的看法。”
vocab.ex_en: Pique at small injuries reveals a heart more concerned with its own importance than with truth.
vocab.ex_zh: 对微小伤害的不满，暴露出一个更关心自己重要而非真理的心。

vocab.def_en: The quality of being easily bent or shaped; flexibility
vocab.quote_en: "and with more quickness of observation and less pliancy of temper than her sister, and with a judgment too unassailed by any attention to herself, she was very little disposed to approve them."
vocab.quote_zh: 她比姐姐观察力更敏锐，性情也不像姐姐那样随和，再加上她本人没有受到任何青睐，因此她对那两位女士颇不以为然。
vocab.ex_en: Pliancy of temper is a gift when it bends toward truth, a weakness when it yields to every wind.
vocab.ex_zh: 性情的柔顺若是向真理弯曲是天赋，若是向每一阵风屈服则是软弱。

vocab.def_en: Done, made, or acting suddenly or without careful consideration
vocab.quote_en: "'And which of the two do you call my little recent piece of modesty?' 'The indirect boast;—for you are really proud of your defects in writing, because you consider them as proceeding from a rapidity of thought and carelessness of execution, which if not estimable, you think at least highly interesting.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “那么我最近那点儿小小谦虚又该算是哪一种呢？”“间接的自夸；因为你实在是以自己写作方面的缺点而自豪，你认为这些缺点是由于思想敏捷和行事潦草所致，你认为即使不值得一提，至少也非常有趣。”
vocab.ex_en: Precipitate judgment is the enemy of truth, for truth requires time to reveal itself.
vocab.ex_zh: 仓促的判断是真理的敌人，因为真理需要时间显现。

vocab.def_en: A preconceived opinion; prejudice
vocab.quote_en: "From herself to Jane—from Jane to Bingley, her thoughts were in a line which soon brought to her recollection that Mr. Darcy's explanation there had appeared very insufficient; and she read it again. Widely different was the effect of a second perusal."
vocab.quote_zh: 从她自己想到吉英，再从吉英想到彬格莱，她的思想联成了一条直线，使她立刻想起了达西先生对这件事的解释非常不够；于是她又重新把信读了一遍。
vocab.ex_en: Prepossession blinds us to evidence that contradicts what we wish to believe.
vocab.ex_zh: 先入之见使我们无视与自己愿意相信之事相悖的证据。

vocab.def_en: To obtain or get with care or effort
vocab.quote_en: "'Why should you be surprised, my dear Eliza?—Do you think it incredible that Mr. Collins should be able to procure any woman's good opinion, because he was not so happy as to succeed with you?'"
vocab.quote_zh: “你为什么这样惊奇，亲爱的伊莱札？你以为柯林斯先生没能得到你的青睐，就得不到任何女人的好感了吗？”
vocab.ex_en: What we procure without effort we value without depth; what costs us much becomes part of us.
vocab.ex_zh: 不费力气得到的东西，我们不会深加珍惜；付出代价的东西则成为我们的一部分。

vocab.def_en: Remarkably or impressively great in extent, size, or degree
vocab.quote_en: "Elizabeth, in the solitude of her chamber, had to meditate upon Charlotte's degree of contentment, to understand her address in guiding, and composure in bearing with her husband, and to acknowledge that it was all done very well."
vocab.quote_zh: 伊丽莎白独自待在房间里，不由得默默思索着夏绿蒂的满足程度，理解她善于指导的本领，沉着忍受丈夫的气度，她不得不承认一切都做得很好。
vocab.ex_en: A prodigious fortune cannot purchase peace of mind, nor can it compensate for the absence of love.
vocab.ex_zh: 巨大的财富买不到内心的平静，也弥补不了爱的缺失。

vocab.def_en: To a remarkable or impressive degree; enormously
vocab.quote_en: "He is as fine a fellow,' said Mr. Bennet, as soon as they were out of the house, 'as ever I saw. He simpers, and smirks, and makes love to us all. I am prodigiously proud of him.'"
vocab.quote_zh: 他们刚一走出大门，班纳特先生就说：“他真是一个好家伙，我从没见过这样的好人。他傻笑，做媚眼，对我们大家献殷勤。我对他真是得意到极点。”
vocab.ex_en: Those who are prodigiously proud of trifles reveal how little they have to be proud of.
vocab.ex_zh: 对琐事极为骄傲的人，暴露了他们没什么可骄傲的。

vocab.def_en: Competent or skilled in doing or using something
vocab.quote_en: "'Oh! certainly,' cried his faithful assistant, 'no one can be really esteemed accomplished, who does not greatly surpass what is usually met with. A woman must have a thorough knowledge of music, singing, drawing, dancing, and the modern languages, to deserve the word.'"
vocab.quote_zh: “哦！当然，”他的忠实助手叫道，“一个女人必须精通音乐、歌唱、绘画、舞蹈和现代语言，才当得起这个称号。”
vocab.ex_en: To be proficient in many arts is admirable; to be proficient in the art of living is wisdom.
vocab.ex_zh: 精通多种技艺令人钦佩；精通生活的艺术才是智慧。

vocab.def_en: Reckless extravagance or wastefulness
vocab.quote_en: "The extravagance and general profligacy which he scrupled not to lay to Mr. Wickham's charge exceedingly shocked her; the more so, as she could bring no proof of its injustice."
vocab.quote_zh: 他毫不迟疑地指责韦翰先生奢侈浪费，行为放荡，这使她大为震惊，尤其是，她拿不出证据来证明这是冤枉了他。
